—Even the greatest men may face great difficulties. Neither a king’s crown nor his inner goodness would protect him from trouble. Even the greatest men must pray diligently. Let no one expect to benefit from the prayers of the church or their friends if they are able to pray themselves but neglect it. Pray that God would protect him and preserve his life. Pray that God would enable him to continue his work for the public good. We can know that God accepts our spiritual offerings when he kindles a holy fire of devotion and love for God in our souls. Also, pray that the Lord would bless his efforts with success. Our first step toward victory in spiritual conflict is to trust only in God’s mercy and grace; those who trust in themselves will soon fall. Believers triumph through faith in God and his revelation of himself, by which they are distinguished from those who live without God in the world. Those who praise God and his name can also trust in God and his name. This was true when the pride and power of Jewish unbelief and pagan idolatry fell before the sermons and lives of humble believers in Jesus. It is true in every conflict with our spiritual enemies, when we confront them in the name, spirit, and power of Christ; and it will be true on the last day, when the world and its ruler will be brought down, but believers, raised from death through the resurrection of the Lord, will stand and sing his praises in heaven. Let us rejoice in Christ’s salvation; let us raise our banners in the name of the Lord our God, confident that by the strength of his powerful hand we will overcome every enemy.
